title: श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम् ॥

श्रीमदानन्दवीरांजनेयं प्रभुं
लङ्किणीदुर्गसंहारकं हनूमन्तम्।
ज्ञानमुद्रांकितं ब्रह्मचर्यव्रतं
रामदूतं भजे मारुतिम्॥

जय जय जय आञ्जनेयं
जय जय जय हनूमन्तम्।
जय जय जय मारुतिं देवं
जय जय जय बजरङ्गबलिम्॥

श्रीरामचन्द्रस्य दासं भक्तं
वायुपुत्रं महाबलं शूरम्।
सुग्रीवसख्यकरं रघुनाथसेवकं
लक्ष्मणप्राणदायकम्॥

अञ्जनागर्भसम्भूतं केसरीनन्दनं विभुम्।
बालार्कमण्डलग्रासं विद्याव्याकरणोत्तमम्।
बुद्धिमन्तं चिरञ्जीविनं समुद्रलङ्घनं कृतम्॥

दक्षिणाशामुखं गत्वा सागरं शतयोजनम्।
उल्लङ्घ्य सिन्धुं सलिलं लङ्कापुरीमुपागतम्॥

लङ्किणीं ताडयित्वा तां राक्षसीगणमर्दनम्।
अशोकवनिकामध्ये सीतां देवीमपश्यत॥

श्रीरामनामाङ्कितं मुद्रिकां ददौ
जानकीमाश्वासयामास वीरः।
चूडामणिं गृह्य रामार्पणं कृतम्
संसृष्टवाक्यं ददौ मैथिल्याः॥

लङ्कादहनं कृतवान् महाबलः
अक्षकुमारवधं तथा विधाय।
मेघनादबन्धनं विमोचयित्वा
रामसमीपं पुनरागतम्॥

संजीवनीं पर्वतं समानयत्
लक्ष्मणस्य प्राणरक्षणार्थम्।
रामरावणयुद्धे सहायभूतं
विजयश्रीं प्रापयत्कपीन्द्रः॥

रामो राज्यमलभत पुनश्च
हनूमत्सेवया कृतार्थम्।
सीतारामौ सिंहासने स्थितौ
पादमूले हनूमान् विराजते॥

ब्रह्मण्यदेवं वरदं कृपालुं
रोगनाशकरं भयापहम्।
सकलविघ्नविनाशकं ह्यनन्तं
मारुतिं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥

इतीदमाञ्जनेयस्य दण्डकं भक्तिसंयुतम्।
यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वान्कामानवाप्नुयात्॥
आयुरारोग्यमैश्वर्यं बलं पुत्रान्धनं तथा।
हनूमत्प्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिर्भवेद्ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Anjaneya Dandakam ||

Shrimad-ananda-viram-Janeyam Prabhum
Lankini-durga-samharakam Hanumantam.
Jnana-mudrankitam Brahmacharya-vratam
Rama-dutam bhaje Marutim.

Jaya Jaya Jaya Anjaneyam
Jaya Jaya Jaya Hanumantam.
Jaya Jaya Jaya Marutim Devam
Jaya Jaya Jaya Bajrangbalim.

Shri Ramachandrasya dasam bhaktam
Vayu-putram Maha-balam Shuram.
Sugriva-sakhya-karam Raghunatha-sevakam
Lakshmana-prana-dayakam.

Anjana-garbha-sambhutam Kesari-nandanam Vibhum.
Bala-arka-mandala-grasam Vidya-vyakarana-uttamam.
Buddhimantam Chiranjivinam Samudra-langhanam kritam.

Dakshina-asha-mukham gatva Sagaram shata-yojanam.
Ullanghya Sindhum salilam Lanka-purim-upagatam.

Lankinim tadayitva tam Rakshasi-gana-mardanam.
Ashoka-vanika-madhye Sitam Devim-apashyata.

Shri Rama-nama-ankitam mudrikam dadau
Janakim-ashvasayamasa Virah.
Chudamanim grihya Rama-arpanam kritam
Samsrishta-vakyam dadau Maithilyah.

Lanka-dahanam kritavan Maha-balah
Aksha-kumara-vadham tatha vidhaya.
Meghanaada-bandhanam vimochayitva
Rama-samipam punar-agatam.

Sanjivanim parvatam samanayat
Lakshmanasya prana-rakshanartham.
Rama-Ravana-yuddhe sahaya-bhutam
Vijaya-shrim prapayat-Kapindrah.

Ramo rajyam-alabhata punash-cha
Hanumat-sevaya krita-artham.
Sita-Ramau simhasane sthitau
Pada-mule Hanuman virajate.

Brahmanyadevam varadam kripaalum
Roga-nashakaram bhaya-apaham.
Sakala-vighna-vinashakam hy-anantam
Marutim sharanam prapadye.

Iti-idam-Anjaneyasya Dandakam bhakti-samyutam.
Yah pathet pratar-utthaya sarvan-kaman-avapnuyat.
Ayur-arogya-maishwaryam balam putran-dhanam tatha.
Hanumat-prasadena sarva-siddhir-bhaved-dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Anjaneya Dandakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम् में हनुमानजी की महिमा का गद्य-पद्य मिश्रित वर्णन है। श्रीमान आनन्दवीर आञ्जनेय, लङ्किणी-दुर्ग संहारक, ज्ञानमुद्राधारी, ब्रह्मचारी, रामदूत मारुति को प्रणाम। अञ्जना के गर्भ से उत्पन्न, केसरी-नन्दन, बाल सूर्य को मुँह में डालने वाले, विद्या-व्याकरण में निपुण, बुद्धिमान, चिरंजीवी हनुमानजी ने शत-योजन सागर लाँघकर लंका जाकर अशोकवाटिका में सीताजी के दर्शन किए, श्रीराम की मुद्रिका दी, चूडामणि लेकर लौटे। लंका दहन किया, अक्षकुमार का वध किया, मेघनाद के बन्धन से मुक्त हुए। लक्ष्मणजी के प्राण-रक्षा हेतु संजीवनी पर्वत लाए। राम-रावण युद्ध में सहायक बने और विजयश्री दिलाई। सीताराम के सिंहासन पर विराजने पर उनके चरणों में हनुमानजी विराजमान हैं। ब्रह्मण्यदेव, वरदायक, कृपालु, रोगनाशक, भयहारी, समस्त विघ्ननाशक मारुति की शरण ग्रहण करता हूँ। इसे प्रातःकाल पढ़ने से सम्पूर्ण कामनाएँ पूर्ण होती हैं, आयु, आरोग्य, ऐश्वर्य, बल, पुत्र एवं धन प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Comprehensive prayer narrating Hanuman's heroic deeds in continuous prose-verse form
Invokes fulfillment of all desires when recited at dawn
Invokes the blessing of long life, good health, prosperity and strength
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears and obstacles from the devotee's life
Associated in tradition with diseases and protects from evil forces
Especially powerful on Tuesdays and Saturdays
description: Dedicated to Lord Hanuman, the Anjaneya Dandakam (श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम्) is a devotional poem in Dandaka metre revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Traditional (South Indian Tradition), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to comprehensive prayer narrating Hanuman's heroic deeds in continuous prose-verse form, cure diseases and protect from evil forces, and remove all fears and obstacles from the devotee's life. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti and Ram Navami for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Anjaneya Dandakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Anjaneya Dandakam in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Powerful prose-hymn narrating Hanuman's heroic deeds for protection and strength.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Anjaneya Dandakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Anjaneya Dandakam (श्री आञ्जनेय दण्डकम्) is a powerful devotional poem in praise of Lord Hanuman (Anjaneya — son of Anjana), composed in the dandaka-chhandas (a long-meter Sanskrit prosody allowing extended flowing verses without strict syllabic count). It is especially beloved across South India — Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka — where Hanuman dandakams are a major devotional genre, recited by school children, athletes, brahmacharis, and Hanuman bhaktas as a daily morning prayer. The composition narrates Hanuman's heroic Sundara Kanda exploits in continuous prose-verse: his birth from Anjana and Kesari, the swallowing of the rising sun as a child, his mastery of vyakarana and the shastras, his hundred-yojana ocean-leap to Lanka, the slaying of Lankini, the discovery of Sita in Ashoka-vana, the giving of Rama's ring and receiving of Sita's choodamani, the Lanka-dahana, the slaying of Aksha-kumara, the defeat by Meghnad's Brahmastra, the bringing of the Sanjeevani mountain to revive Lakshmana, and his enthronement at Sita-Rama's feet. (Sources: Traditional South Indian Anjaneya Dandakam tradition; Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda parallels.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the dandaka-chhandas (dandaka metre) used in this composition?
seoFaqs.answer: Dandaka is a unique Sanskrit prosodic form allowing extremely long verses — often of 30, 40, 50 or more syllables per line — in which the strict syllabic count of classical chhandas (like anushtup, indravajra, vasantatilaka, mandakranta) is relaxed to allow flowing, semi-prose narrative cadence. The name dandaka comes from danda (staff, rod) — referencing the long unbroken rod-like flow of the verses. Dandaka is especially favoured for narrative-devotional compositions where the poet wishes to recount a hero's deeds in extended sweeping passages without metrical interruption. Famous dandakams in Sanskrit include the Karpura Manjari Dandaka, Mahishasura Mardini Dandaka, the Ramayana's own dandaka-passages in Sundara Kanda, and the Telugu Hanuman dandakams. The dandaka-chhandas is particularly suited to Hanuman because it mirrors his sweeping leaps, sustained battle-deeds, and continuous flow of devotional energy. (Sources: Sanskrit chhandas-shastra; Pingala Chhanda Sutras; Telugu chhandas tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What heroic deeds of Hanuman does the Anjaneya Dandakam narrate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dandakam narrates the complete Sundara Kanda heroic-cycle of Hanuman: (1) his miraculous birth from Anjana and Kesari, fathered by Vayu; (2) his childhood swallowing of the rising sun (bala-arka-mandala-grasam); (3) his mastery of all branches of vyakarana, vidya, and the shastras (vidya-vyakarana-uttamam) — Hanuman is hailed as the foremost grammarian; (4) his chiranjivi (immortal) status; (5) the hundred-yojana ocean-leap (samudra-langhanam) to Lanka; (6) the slaying of the demoness Lankini at Lanka's gate; (7) the discovery of Sita in the Ashoka-vana; (8) the giving of Rama's name-engraved ring (mudrika) and receiving of Sita's chudamani jewel; (9) the destruction-by-fire of Lanka (Lanka-dahanam); (10) the slaying of the asura Aksha-kumara; (11) his temporary capture by Meghnad's Brahmastra and self-release; (12) the bringing of the entire Sanjeevani mountain from the Himalayas to revive the unconscious Lakshmana; (13) his role as the indispensable ally in the Rama-Ravana war; (14) his eternal seat at the lotus-feet of the enthroned Sita-Rama. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; traditional Anjaneya Dandakam.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Anjaneya Dandakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Anjaneya Dandakam is most powerful when recited at dawn (pratah-utthaya) on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) — the two principal days of Hanuman worship, especially Saturday for relief from Shani-affliction (Hanuman is traditionally the protector against Shani). The most auspicious days of the year are: Hanuman Jayanti (in north India: Chaitra Purnima; in south India: Vaishakha Krishna Dashami called Hanuman Jayanti in Andhra/Telangana, Margashirsha Amavasya in Tamil Nadu), Ram Navami, Diwali night Hanuman puja, and during the entire 41-day Hanuman Mandala vrat. Devotees recite it before the Hanuman idol after offering vermilion (sindoor), jasmine garland (malliga-puvvu), butter (venna), and tulsi-banana naivedya. Athletes, students, brahmacharis, and Sanskrit-Telugu-Tamil scholars chant it daily for vidya, bala, and ojas. (Sources: Drikpanchang Hanuman Jayanti regional dates; traditional Anjaneya upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Anjaneya Dandakam different from the Hanuman Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are powerful Hanuman devotional compositions but differ in language, length, metre, and devotional milieu. The Hanuman Chalisa is a 40-verse Awadhi devotional composition by Sant Tulsidas (1532-1623 CE) in the Doha-Chaupai metre, accessible to non-Sanskrit devotees and especially loved across north India in Hindi-speaking regions; it follows a praise-petition-protection structure with the famous Sankat Mochan epithet. The Anjaneya Dandakam is a Sanskrit prose-verse composition in the long-flowing dandaka-chhandas, especially loved in South India — Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Karnataka — where Telugu and Tamil regional versions are also widespread; it is more narrative and scholarly, recounting the entire Sundara Kanda. Devotees often recite both daily — the Chalisa for north Indian Awadhi-bhakti tradition, the Dandakam for south Indian Sanskrit-Telugu-Tamil tradition. (Sources: Hanuman Chalisa by Tulsidas; traditional South Indian Anjaneya Dandakam.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of the Anjaneya Dandakam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Iti-idam-Anjaneyasya Dandakam bhakti-samyutam, yah pathet pratar-utthaya sarvan-kaman-avapnuyat, ayur-arogya-maishwaryam balam putran-dhanam tatha, Hanumat-prasadena sarva-siddhir-bhaved-dhruvam — the one who recites this Anjaneya Dandakam at dawn with devotion obtains: ayur (long life), arogya (good health), aishvarya (prosperity), bala (strength), putra (children) and dhana (wealth) — all desires fulfilled, and assured siddhi through Hanuman's grace. Tradition further attributes: (1) protection from all bhaya (fears) — bhaya-apaha; (2) protection from all rogas (diseases) — roga-nashakara; (3) destruction of all vighnas (obstacles) — sakala-vighna-vinashaka; (4) protection from evil forces, ghosts, sorcery, and asura-energies; (5) Shani-relief on Saturdays (Hanuman as Shani-rakshaka); (6) special grace for students of Sanskrit grammar and the shastras (vidya-vyakarana-uttamam); (7) brahmacharya-shakti for the celibate; (8) bala for the warrior, athlete, and military person; (9) ultimate Rama-bhakti and entrance into the Rama-darbar at Hanuman's feet. (Sources: Anjaneya Dandakam phalashruti; Valmiki Ramayana Hanuman Mahatmya.)
